title: Welcome to C 🚀
description: Discover the mother of all modern programming languages.

slides.title: Meet C 🦕
slides.content:
## The Grandfather of Languages

Created in **1972** by Dennis Ritchie at Bell Labs, **C** is one of the oldest and most influential programming languages in the world.

It is often called the **"mother of all languages"** because many modern languages (like C++, Java, JavaScript, Python, and C#) borrow heavily from its syntax and concepts.

slides.title: Why Learn C? 🏎️
slides.content:
So, why learn a language that's over 50 years old?

* **Speed & Efficiency**: C is incredibly fast and uses minimal memory.
* **Close to the Metal**: It gives you direct access to the computer's memory and hardware.
* **Foundational Knowledge**: Learning C teaches you *how computers actually work* under the hood.

quizData.question: Why is C often referred to as the 'mother of all languages'?

options.text: It is the only language computers understand.
It naturally speaks to the motherboard.
Many modern languages are based on its syntax and concepts.

quizData.explanation: Languages like C++, Java, and Python inherited many features and syntax rules from C.

slides.title: Where is C Used? 🌍
slides.content:
C is everywhere! Because of its high performance, it is used to build:

* **Operating Systems**: Windows, Linux, and macOS kernels are written in C.
* **Embedded Systems**: Microwaves, smartwatches, and car engines.
* **Game Engines**: The core engines of many high-performance games.
* **Other Languages**: The Python and PHP interpreters are written in C!

quizData.question: Which of the following is NOT typically built using C?
quizData.explanation: C is a low-level systems language. Web pages are typically built using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content:
## A Steep but Worthwhile Climb

C is stricter than languages like Python. It requires you to manage memory manually and define variable types explicitly. It might feel challenging at first, but it will make you a much stronger programmer in the long run!
